ucs.title: [LLM] SideCopy (G1008) LNK→mshta.exe remote-HTA stager fetching second-stage from attacker infrastructure
ucs.description: Hunts SideCopy's signature initial-access chain — a spearphishing archive containing an LNK that invokes mshta.exe with a remote HTTP/HTTPS URL to fetch a malicious HTA stager (the technique it borrowed from / mimics Sidewinder, hence the name). The combination of mshta with an external URL parented by explorer.exe / archive extractors / Office is highly specific to this Pakistan-nexus crew.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.dest) as host from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="mshta.exe" (Processes.process="*http://*" OR Processes.process="*https://*" OR Processes.process="*\\\\*") (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("explorer.exe","winrar.exe","7zfm.exe","7zg.exe","outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=cmdline "(?<remote_url>https?://[^\s\"']+|\\\\\\\\[^\s\"']+)" | where isnotnull(remote_url) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// SideCopy G1008 — mshta.exe loading a remote HTA (T1218.005) parented by explorer/archive/Office
let _archive_or_office = dynamic(["explorer.exe","winrar.exe","7zfm.exe","7zg.exe","outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","thunderbird.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName =~ "mshta.exe"
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
// SideCopy stagers always pass an http(s) URL or UNC path as the first arg to mshta
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","\\\\")
// parent narrows to user-driven launches typical of LNK-from-archive opens
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_archive_or_office)
| extend RemoteUrl = extract(@"(?i)(https?://[^\s\"']+|\\\\[^\s\"']+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
// pivot: did mshta then talk out to that destination? high-confidence chain
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "mshta.exe"
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl_net = RemoteUrl
  ) on DeviceId
| where isnull(NetTime) or NetTime between (Timestamp .. Timestamp + 2m)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          MshtaCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          RemoteUrl, NetTime, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl_net,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// SideCopy G1008 — mshta.exe with remote URL spawned by explorer/archive/Office (4688)
let _archive_or_office = dynamic(["explorer.exe","winrar.exe","7zfm.exe","7zg.exe","outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","thunderbird.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\mshta.exe"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where CommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","\\\\")
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in (_archive_or_office)
| extend RemoteUrl = extract(@"(?i)(https?://[^\s\"']+|\\\\[^\s\"']+)", 1, CommandLine)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, RemoteUrl, ProcessId, NewProcessId
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: SideCopy mshta.exe remote HTA stager from archive or Office parent
id: 8e7d4b1c-3a2f-4f4d-9c1d-sidecopy-g1008-uc1
status: experimental
description: Detects SideCopy (G1008) initial access where an LNK delivered via spearphishing archive launches mshta.exe with a remote HTTP(S) URL or UNC path. Parented by explorer.exe / archive utilities / Office / browser / mail client.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1008/
  - https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2021/12/sidecopy-apt-targets-india
author: detection-engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1218.005
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.g1008
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith: '\mshta.exe'
  selection_remote:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
      - '\\\\'
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\winrar.exe'
      - '\7zfm.exe'
      - '\7zg.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: all of selection_* and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Legacy enterprise apps that legitimately deploy HTA from intranet shares (allowlist by URL/UNC)
level: high
ucs.rationale: SideCopy's 2019-present infection chain is consistently: spearphish ZIP/RAR → LNK → mshta.exe https://<staged-domain>/<file>.hta → HTA drops the side-loading pair. Generic mshta detections fire on legacy admin scripts; demanding (a) a remote URL/UNC argument AND (b) a user-shell / archive / Office / browser parent surgically isolates the SideCopy pattern (and other LNK-borne mshta abusers like Sid

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `RemoteUrl` is not a column of SecurityEvent; it does exist on DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceEvents, AlertEvidence, ASCDeviceEvents.

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] SideCopy (G1008) DLL side-loading: signed-binary loader running from user-writable path with co-located unsigned DLL
ucs.description: Hunts SideCopy's hallmark second-stage delivery — they consistently drop a legitimate signed binary (Microsoft/Adobe/Citrix/Credential Manager helpers) into %TEMP% / %APPDATA% / %PUBLIC% alongside a malicious DLL whose name matches an import the binary search-order-loads. Catches Action RAT, ReverseRAT, AllaKore RAT delivery without relying on hash IOCs.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as binary_path values(Filesystem.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*") Filesystem.file_name IN ("DUser.dll","credwiz.exe","CredentialUIBroker.exe","WerFault.exe","taskhostw.exe","msiexec.exe","DismHost.exe","version.dll","vcruntime140.dll","WINMM.dll","DUI70.dll") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | join type=inner dest process_guid [| tstats `summariesonly` count values(Processes.process_name) as loader values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process) as cmdline from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" by Processes.dest Processes.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// SideCopy G1008 — DLL side-loading: signed Microsoft/Adobe binary in user dir loading
// an unsigned/different-author DLL from the SAME directory (T1574.001)
let _user_writable = dynamic([@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Downloads\"]);
// SideCopy-favoured signed-binary lures (Action/Reverse/AllaKore loader vehicles)
let _abused_loaders = dynamic(["credwiz.exe","CredentialUIBroker.exe","DismHost.exe","WerFault.exe","taskhostw.exe","msiexec.exe","PrintIsolationHost.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","DUser.dll-host"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
// loader binary itself dropped into a user-writable path
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_user_writable)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_abused_loaders)
   or InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any ("Microsoft Corporation","Adobe","Citrix","VMware")
// loaded module is also from the same user-writable path = classic side-load
| where FolderPath has_any (_user_writable)
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
// strip noise: same publisher / system DLLs / matching SHA256 (digest match = same file)
| where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256
| where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          LoaderImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          LoaderPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          LoaderCompany = InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName,
          LoaderProduct = InitiatingProcessVersionInfoProductName,
          LoaderSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          LoaderCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          DllName = FileName, DllPath = FolderPath, DllSHA256 = SHA256, DllSize = FileSize,
          User = InitiatingProcessAccountName
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FolderPath has_any (_user_writable)
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, DllPath = FolderPath, DllName = FileName,
              DropProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName, DropParent = InitiatingProcessParentFileName
  ) on DeviceName, DllName, DllPath
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// SideCopy G1008 — DLL side-load chain via Sysmon EID 7 (ImageLoad)
// requires Sysmon with EID 7 enabled on endpoints; falls back to ImFileEvent for the drop side
let _user_writable = dynamic([@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Downloads\"]);
let _abused_loaders = dynamic(["credwiz.exe","CredentialUIBroker.exe","DismHost.exe","WerFault.exe","taskhostw.exe","msiexec.exe","PrintIsolationHost.exe","vmtoolsd.exe"]);
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7
| extend Image = tostring(EventData.Image),
         ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
         Signed = tostring(EventData.Signed),
         SignatureStatus = tostring(EventData.SignatureStatus),
         Hashes = tostring(EventData.Hashes),
         User = tostring(EventData.User),
         Company = tostring(EventData.Company)
| where Image has_any (_user_writable)
| extend LoaderLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(Image, "\\")[-1]))
| where LoaderLeaf in~ (_abused_loaders) or Company has_any ("Microsoft","Adobe","Citrix","VMware")
| where ImageLoaded has_any (_user_writable)
| where ImageLoaded endswith ".dll"
| where ImageLoaded !startswith @"C:\Windows\"
// flag if loaded DLL is unsigned or signature invalid
| where Signed =~ "false" or SignatureStatus !~ "Valid" or isempty(SignatureStatus)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, User, Image, ImageLoaded, Company, Signed, SignatureStatus, Hashes
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: SideCopy's loader trinity (Action RAT, ReverseRAT, AllaKore RAT) is consistently delivered via DLL side-loading: a signed first-party binary is dropped to %TEMP% / Public / ProgramData and resolves a malicious adjacent DLL on import. Detecting this via image-load + path predicate (rather than file hashes) is durable across SideCopy campaigns because they rotate the malicious DLLs but keep re-using
